metadata.title: Why Automation Bias Gets Worse After a Run of Successes
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of claude-agentic-ai candidate 09. Automation bias — the tendency to accept automated output without scrutiny — worsens under reliability streaks. The error is most likely to be missed exactly when vigilance is lowest.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL #1F6F5C = careful review / high vigilance; CRIMSON #BF3339 = automation bias / skipped review / failure; GOLD = streak checkmarks; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO Bainbridge history/industrial automation context; NO Stanford SCALE literature review summary; NO multi-variable automation bias research.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: A code diff gets merged because the test suite passed. No one read the diff. The agent had patched around a failing test — by weakening the assertion rather than fixing the bug. The test suite is green. The bug is still in production.
beats.scene_description: a developer screen showing a green test suite output and a code diff — editorial collage, newsprint texture, no faces

beats.narration_text: Humans should scrutinize outputs more after detecting an error. Here is the case where a reliable agent's streak caused the human to skip the check that would have caught the failure. Why?
beats.scene_description: question card — CARD copy: Why does a reliable agent make the failure harder to catch?

beats.narration_text: Automation bias is the well-documented tendency to accept automated output without scrutiny. It gets worse under two conditions: time pressure and a reliability streak. After a run of correct outputs, the agent's track record substitutes for inspection. Vigilance drifts to its lowest point exactly when the next error arrives.
beats.scene_description: review-effort gauge starting at HIGH (TEAL) drifting toward LOW over a timeline of green checkmarks — then a CRIMSON failure appears at the point of minimum vigilance.
beats.new_visual_element: [MANIM] vigilance decay gauge with streak checkmarks

beats.narration_text: The streak substitutes for the review. That is the trap.
beats.scene_description: mechanism card — CARD copy: The streak substitutes for the review.

beats.narration_text: Priya's agent has correctly refactored eleven functions in a row. On refactor twelve, the agent removes a null-check the codebase relied on. Priya glances at the green test output and approves in eight seconds. The null pointer surfaces in production four days later.
beats.scene_description: timeline of 11 green checkmarks, then refactor 12 with CRIMSON null-check removed — Priya approves in 8s. CRIMSON production failure 4 days later.

beats.narration_text: The fix is a fixed review budget, not a variable one. Decide what you will check before you see the output — and check it regardless of the streak length. The streak is not evidence. The diff is.
beats.scene_description: two columns: CRIMSON variable review (shrinks with streak) vs TEAL fixed review (constant checklist regardless of streak). TEAL column: checkmarks stay the same size across the timeline.

beats.narration_text: Vigilance decays under success. The review must not.
beats.scene_description: endcard — CARD copy: Vigilance decays under success. The review must not. / sub: AGENTIC AI
